commands.review_summary:
The PR changes systemd gateway scope selection and activation paths to detect same-name user/system unit conflicts, prefer the active/enabled system unit, retire the opposite-scope unit, and adds regression tests in the systemd daemon test file.

PR surface: Source +117, Tests +154. Total +271 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, but not by live execution in this review: current main returns the user-scope unit before considering an active/enabled system unit, and restart/install paths act on that selected scope. The linked issue also provides concrete upgrade steps and logs, but the PR still needs real after-fix proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should review the service-retirement policy and require real Linux systemd proof; there is no narrow code defect for ClawSweeper to repair automatically from this review.

target.title: Fix systemd gateway unit scope conflicts
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #79375.

- Detect same-name user-scope and system-scope systemd gateway units and prefer the active/enabled system unit instead of blindly selecting the user unit.
- Retire the opposite-scope unit before service activation/control paths that would otherwise create dueling gateway services on the same port.
- Add regression coverage for conflict detection, install-time system-unit retirement, and restart-time user-unit retirement.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The systemd gateway scope resolver treated a user-scope unit file as authoritative whenever it existed, even if a same-name system-scope OpenClaw gateway unit was active or enabled.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch changes the owner decision for the conflicting-unit state and retires the stale opposite-scope unit at the activation/control boundary, so OpenClaw no longer starts or restarts two default gateway services against the same port.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Upgrades or repairs that leave both user-scope and system-scope `openclaw-gateway.service` units no longer let both services manage the same gateway port.
- **Real environment tested:** Not yet run on the live Telegram/systemd host in this checkout.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Pending external proof on another machine after this branch is pushed.
- **Evidence after fix:** Pending external proof.
- **Observed result after fix:** Pending external proof.
- **What was not tested:** Live Telegram bot response and real systemd user/system unit migration on the target Linux host were intentionally deferred until after branch push.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/systemd.test.ts`
  - pass: 1 test file passed, 91 tests passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
  - pass: no TypeScript errors reported.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/daemon/systemd.ts src/daemon/systemd.test.ts`
  - pass: both changed files use the correct format.
- `git diff --check`
  - pass: no whitespace errors reported.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/daemon/systemd.test.ts`
- **Fix classification:** root-cause systemd service conflict fix
- **Patch quality notes:** The diff stays in the systemd service owner and its tests; it does not change Telegram message handling or stale PID matching.

## Merge risk

- **Why it matters / User impact:** A dueling user/system service pair can keep killing the gateway, causing channels such as Telegram to stop responding and to hit API rate limits during repeated restarts.
- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk:service-management`, `merge-risk:linux-systemd`
- **Risk explanation:** The change stops/disables conflicting same-name systemd units. Hosts intentionally running multiple gateways should use separate unit names, ports, config, and state.
- **Why acceptable:** The default `openclaw-gateway.service` unit represents one managed gateway. Keeping two same-name units alive is the failure mode in this issue, not a supported multi-gateway setup.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** blocked on live proof; local unit tests, typecheck, format, and diff checks pass.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
